without_eeg.description: Transformer DQN with [y, z, ty, tz, dist] state only

with_eeg.description: Transformer DQN with [y, z, ty, tz, dist, eeg_pred, confidence] state

conclusion.finding: Both agents achieve near-perfect control (>98% reach rate). The EEG-aware agent trains faster (668s vs 1130s) and reaches 100% during training, indicating that EEG predictions provide useful directional hints that accelerate learning. At evaluation time, both converge to similar performance, confirming that the RL agent can learn effective policies regardless of EEG input availability.
conclusion.eeg_classification_to_control: Even with 82.22% EEG classification accuracy (not perfect), the RL agent achieves >98% control success rate, demonstrating that closed-loop RL effectively compensates for classification errors.
